audit_items.proof_trace: q6apm_graph_open() at L257 allocates a graph resource that requires an explicit q6apm_graph_close() to release (as confirmed by q6apm_dai_close). On the four error paths after the graph is successfully opened (L271-274, L279-282, L286-289, L291-295), the single 'err:' label at L305 only calls kfree(prtd) and never calls q6apm_graph_close(prtd->graph), leaking the graph object. runtime->private_data is set only at L297 (after all constraints), so the ALSA close callback cannot recover the leaked resource.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A process with access to the ALSA audio device opens a stream such that q6apm_graph_open succeeds but a subsequent snd_pcm_hw_constraint_* call fails (e.g., under memory pressure); repeated open attempts produce unbounded kernel graph resource accumulation.
